title: DMC-12 Error Taxonomy v0.1.0
description: Shape of the error object a DMC-12 tool returns in the response body (the `data` member of the standard envelope) when a call fails. Cross-cutting — applies to every capability, not a single tool surface. The taxonomy structure (a compact code set + a `retryable` flag + a correlation `error_id`) is informed by the Auto Agent Protocol (AAP, https://autoagentprotocol.org, Apache-2.0); DMC-12 keeps its own code names. Additive + non-breaking: the legacy `error` string is preserved alongside the taxonomy fields, so pre-taxonomy consumers reading `error` keep working.

ErrorCode.description: Stable, documented DMC-12 error code. Implementations map their internal error strings onto exactly one of these.

error.description: Legacy machine-readable error string. Stable per implementation and PRESERVED for backward compatibility — consumers written before the taxonomy read this. New consumers SHOULD switch on `error_code`.

error_code.description: The DMC-12 error code this error maps to. The recommended field to branch on.

retryable.description: Whether retrying the identical request could plausibly succeed later. true for transient classes (RATE_LIMITED, UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE, INTERNAL_ERROR); false for deterministic ones (NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_FAILED, NOT_AUTHORIZED, state/expiry errors).

error_id.description: Opaque per-error correlation id (e.g. `err_<uuid4hex>`) for support / log correlation. Unique per occurrence; safe to surface to an end user or quote back to the merchant.

message.description: Optional human-readable, non-sensitive detail. MUST NOT contain PII or secrets.

allOf.$comment: retryable is FIXED by error_code (SPEC §8 / capabilities/errors.md): the transient classes are retryable: true, every other code is retryable: false. This conditional makes a conformance validator reject contradictory pairings (e.g. RATE_LIMITED + retryable:false).

examples.message: rate limit exceeded; retry after the window resets
